title: Decide peer-reported location privacy semantics
description: Why this exists: network-derived location may come from peer- or mesh-reported coordinates, but treating peer reports as this node location is privacy- and semantics-sensitive. What needs to be done: decide whether peer-reported coordinates can ever become this node NETWORK location, define provenance/source_name rules, decide trust/staleness/epoch policy alignment with build/provision floors, and add protocol vectors/docs before production ingestion.
notes:
Decision implemented in docs/spec/vector descriptions: announce app_data coordinates are peer-owned routing metadata. A receiver MUST NOT publish another peer's coordinates as this node's local physical position by default. Gateways MAY publish an approximate mesh-derived local NETWORK location only under explicit local policy; it must preserve NETWORK provenance/source_name such as mesh-announce, age out with announce freshness, remain lower priority than fresh manual/static or local-client sources, and must not upgrade peer fix source to local GNSS/manual/local-client. Added follow-up project-LICHEN-ua1r to gate the current announce-derived NETWORK fallback before production L2 dispatch; project-LICHEN-ua1r now blocks project-LICHEN-9c0g.
Review outcomes: Bohr the 9th found P2 explicit fallback policy not implemented in current gateway code/tests; Goodall the 9th escalated same issue to P1 production blocker because announce_ingest/network_location are compiled and publish mesh-announce unconditionally when invoked. Disposition: created/updated blocker project-LICHEN-ua1r at P1 and made it block project-LICHEN-9c0g production dispatch. This decision Bead remains docs/vector semantics only; implementation gate is tracked separately before production RX wiring can close.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware peer-reported location privacy decision. Updated spec/05-routing.md, docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md, and announce coordinate vector descriptions to define announce coordinates as peer-owned routing metadata; local NETWORK publication is approximate, opt-in, provenance-preserving, stale-bound, lower priority than fresh local providers, and coordinate-only metadata carries no Unix fix time. Reviews: Bohr found P2 missing policy gate; Goodall escalated to P1 production blocker; Halley identified epoch/local-provider wording gaps. Fixed wording gaps and filed/upgraded project-LICHEN-ua1r as P1 blocker for production dispatch project-LICHEN-9c0g. Validation: python3 test/vectors/generate.py plus git diff --check passed; cd python && uv run pytest -q tests/test_vectors.py passed 105/105. Ruff on full generator was attempted but hit existing unrelated baseline E501/W191 issues outside this diff.
